q: Thought is so cunning, so clever, that it distorts everything for its own convenience.
h:
“Thought is so cunning, so clever, that it distorts everything for its own convenience.” —
Jiddu Krishnamurti

q: It is secondary whether we choose belief or defiance. What is precious is that we are always able to choose.
h:
“It is secondary whether we choose belief or defiance. What is precious is that we are always able to choose.” —
Ming-Dao Deng

q: The traveler sees what he sees. The tourist sees what he has come to see.
h:
“The traveler sees what he sees. The tourist sees what he has come to see.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: You can't build a reputation on what you are going to do.
h:
“You can't build a reputation on what you are going to do. ” —
Henry Ford

q: Much learning does not teach understanding.
h:
“Much learning does not teach understanding.” —
Heraclitus

q: The only place success comes before work is in the dictionary.
h:
“The only place success comes before work is in the dictionary.” —
Vince Lombardi

q: Perplexity is the beginning of knowledge.
h:
“Perplexity is the beginning of knowledge.” —
Kahlil Gibran

q: Courage is knowing what not to fear.
h:
“Courage is knowing what not to fear.” —
Plato

q: When you have vision it affects your attitude. Your attitude is optimistic rather than pessimistic.
h:
“When you have vision it affects your attitude. Your attitude is optimistic rather than pessimistic. ” —
Charles Swindoll

q: No matter who you are, we're creatures of habit. The better your habits are, the better they will be in pressure situations.
h:
“No matter who you are, we're creatures of habit. The better your habits are, the better they will be in pressure situations.” —
Wayne Gretzky

q: Not doing it is certainly the best way to not getting it.
h:
“Not doing it is certainly the best way to not getting it. ” —
Wayne Gretzky

q: There are people who have money and people who are rich.
h:
“There are people who have money and people who are rich.” —
Coco Chanel

q: If you can't fly, run. If you can't run, walk. If you can't walk, crawl, but by all means, keep moving.
h:
“If you can't fly, run. If you can't run, walk. If you can't walk, crawl, but by all means, keep moving.” —
Martin Luther King, Jr.

q: The universe doesn't allow perfection.
h:
“The universe doesn't allow perfection.” —
Stephen Hawking

q: Formal education will make you a living, self-education will make you a fortune.
h:
“Formal education will make you a living, self-education will make you a fortune.” —
Jim Rohn

q: Your greatest asset is your earning ability. Your greatest resource is your time.
h:
“Your greatest asset is your earning ability. Your greatest resource is your time.” —
Brian Tracy

q: Man is affected not by events but by the view he takes of them.
h:
“Man is affected not by events but by the view he takes of them. ” —
Seneca

q: If you are not living each day with excitement, energy, and passion, then you are not living true to your life purpose.
h:
“If you are not living each day with excitement, energy, and passion, then you are not living true to your life purpose.” —
Celestine Chua

q: Truth is exact correspondence with reality.
h:
“Truth is exact correspondence with reality. ” —
Paramahansa Yogananda

q: When the world pushes you to your knees, you're in the perfect position to pray.
h:
“When the world pushes you to your knees, you're in the perfect position to pray.” —
Rumi

q: People don't care how much you know until they know how much you care.
h:
“People don't care how much you know until they know how much you care.” —
Theodore Roosevelt

q: Ideas do not reach perfection in a day, no matter how much study is put upon them.
h:
“Ideas do not reach perfection in a day, no matter how much study is put upon them.” —
Alexander Graham Bell

q: If you're really truthful with yourself, it's a wonderful guidance.
h:
“If you're really truthful with yourself, it's a wonderful guidance. ” —
Charlie Chaplin

q: Opportunities multiply as they are seized.
h:
“Opportunities multiply as they are seized.” —
Sun Tzu

q: Art, like morality, consists of drawing the line somewhere.
h:
“Art, like morality, consists of drawing the line somewhere.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: People who belittle people, will be LITTLE people, and will accomplish very Little
h:
“People who belittle people, will be LITTLE people, and will accomplish very Little” —
Norman Vincent Peale

q: New opinions are always suspected, and usually opposed, without any other reason but because they are not common.
h:
“New opinions are always suspected, and usually opposed, without any other reason but because they are not common.” —
John Locke

q: I think it's very important to have a feedback loop, where you're constantly thinking about what you've done and how you could be doing it better.
h:
“I think it's very important to have a feedback loop, where you're constantly thinking about what you've done and how you could be doing it better.” —
Elon Musk

q: It's only after you've stepped outside your comfort zone that you begin to change, grow, and transform.
h:
“It's only after you've stepped outside your comfort zone that you begin to change, grow, and transform.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: What is a friend? A single soul dwelling in two bodies.
h:
“What is a friend? A single soul dwelling in two bodies.” —
Aristotle

q: Those who realize their folly are not true fools.
h:
“Those who realize their folly are not true fools.” —
Zhuangzi

q: With the new day comes new strength and new thoughts.
h:
“With the new day comes new strength and new thoughts.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: I am who I am today because of the choices I made yesterday.
h:
“I am who I am today because of the choices I made yesterday.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: Life is what happens to you while you're busy making other plans.
h:
“Life is what happens to you while you're busy making other plans.” —
John Lennon

q: True friendship ought never to conceal what it thinks.
h:
“True friendship ought never to conceal what it thinks. ” —
St. Jerome

q: Poverty and hardship are created by false thinking.
h:
“Poverty and hardship are created by false thinking.” —
Bodhidharma

q: If you're brave enough to say goodbye, life will reward you with a new hello.
h:
“If you're brave enough to say goodbye, life will reward you with a new hello.” —
Paulo Coelho

q: Change is not a four letter word...but often your reaction to it is!
h:
“Change is not a four letter word...but often your reaction to it is!” —
Jeffrey Gitomer

q: A Hero is an ordinary individual who finds the strength to persevere and endure in spite of overwhelming obstacles.
h:
“A Hero is an ordinary individual who finds the strength to persevere and endure in spite of overwhelming obstacles.” —
Christopher Reeve

q: If you focus on results and finding shortcuts, you'll get impatient. If you focus on the process and doing the right thing, you'll be unstoppable.
h:
“If you focus on results and finding shortcuts, you'll get impatient. If you focus on the process and doing the right thing, you'll be unstoppable.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: When you've got nothing, you've got nothing to lose.
h:
“When you've got nothing, you've got nothing to lose.” —
Bob Dylan

q: Work as though you would live forever, and live as though you would die today. Go another mile.
h:
“Work as though you would live forever, and live as though you would die today. Go another mile.” —
Og Mandino

q: It doesn't matter if the glass is half empty or half full. Be grateful you have a glass - you're the only person that can decide what's in it.
h:
“It doesn't matter if the glass is half empty or half full. Be grateful you have a glass - you're the only person that can decide what's in it.” —
Gurbaksh Chahal

q: There is no path to Peace. Peace is the path.
h:
“There is no path to Peace. Peace is the path.” —
Dan Millman

q: Good things aren't supposed to just fall into your lap.
h:
“Good things aren't supposed to just fall into your lap.” —
Audrey Hepburn

q: Turn off your mind, relax, and float downstream.
h:
“Turn off your mind, relax, and float downstream.” —
John Lennon

q: Peace is letting it be. Letting life flow, letting emotions flow through you.
h:
“Peace is letting it be. Letting life flow, letting emotions flow through you.” —
Kamal Ravikant

q: You Create Your Own Present By What You Give Your Attention To Today.
h:
“You Create Your Own Present By What You Give Your Attention To Today.” —
Spencer Johnson

q: It is amazing what you can accomplish if you do not care who gets the credit.
h:
“It is amazing what you can accomplish if you do not care who gets the credit.” —
Harry S. Truman

q: It's wise to tell the truth. It's even wiser to tell it kindly.
h:
“It's wise to tell the truth. It's even wiser to tell it kindly.” —
Maxime Lagace
